item.title: 13 Flowering Plants Rabbits Will Leave Alone
item.description:
Want to close down the bunny salad bar in your garden? Grow flowering plants rabbits don’t like to eat! These annuals and perennials are beautiful, colorful, and rabbit-resistant. A few, like thyme and chives, are also delicious – to humans. Hop along, bunny, the flowering plants in this garden taste bad to rabbits.

The post 13 Flowering Plants Rabbits Will Leave Alone appeared first on Gardener's Path.
item.source:markdown:
Want to close down the bunny salad bar in your garden? Grow flowering plants rabbits don’t like to eat! These annuals and perennials are beautiful, colorful, and rabbit-resistant. item.title: Learn How to Grow and Care for Birch Trees
item.description:
Birch trees bring year-round beauty to the landscape with their distinctive papery bark, delicate dancing leaves, and golden fall color. From the white-barked paper birch to the heat-tolerant river birch, there's a species for nearly every climate. Learn how to grow and care for birch trees in this guide. Read more now.

The post Learn How to Grow and Care for Birch Trees appeared first on Gardener's Path.
item.source:markdown:
Birch trees bring year-round beauty to the landscape with their distinctive papery bark, delicate dancing leaves, and golden fall color. From the white-barked paper birch to the heat-tolerant river birch, there's a species for nearly every climate. Learn how to grow and care for birch trees in this guide. Read more now.

item.title: Learn How to Grow and Care for New Zealand Flax
item.description:
With long, sword-like and richly colored leaves, New Zealand flax develops into large, vibrant clusters of bold structural growth for both sun and shade gardens. Grown mainly for the foliage, mature plants also produce tall scapes of bright tubular flowers. Learn how to grow New Zealand flax in this guide. Read more now.

The post Learn How to Grow and Care for New Zealand Flax appeared first on Gardener's Path.

item.title: Signs your avocado tree is healthy . . . or not
item.description:
May is usually an easy time of year for an avocado tree in Southern California. Therefore, signs of ill health now should be taken seriously and, if possible, remedied before the potential stress of heat or drought during summer arrives. In my yard this May I have some healthy and some not -so-healthy avocado trees. […]

The post Signs your avocado tree is healthy . . . or not appeared first on Greg Alder's Yard Posts: Southern California food gardening.
item.source:markdown:
May is usually an easy time of year for an avocado tree in Southern California. Therefore, signs of ill health now should be taken seriously and, if possible, remedied before the potential stress of heat or drought during summer arrives. In my yard this May I have some healthy and some not -so-healthy avocado trees. \[…\]

item.title: Blueberry Recipes to Make During California Blueberry Season
item.description:
California blueberry season is one of spring’s sweetest signals. In California, blueberry harvest typically runs April through June, and because blueberries are allowed to ripen on the plant, growers harvest them multiple times throughout the season. That means spring is the perfect time to look for fresh California blueberries at the grocery store, farmers market […]

The post Blueberry Recipes to Make During California Blueberry Season appeared first on California Grown.
item.source:markdown:
California blueberry season is one of spring’s sweetest signals. That means spring is the perfect time to look for fresh California blueberries at the grocery store, farmers market \[…\]

item.title: How to Plant and Grow Red Currants
item.description:
Red currants are flavorful and full of nutrients. There are lots of species and cultivars to choose from of this underappreciated beauty, whether you need some bright autumn color, love vibrant spring blossoms, or want to enjoy the berries in tasty desserts and savory meals. Learn how to grow red currants in this guide.

item.title: Are Leafy Greens with Insect Holes Safe to Eat?
item.description:
Are leafy greens with bug holes okay to eat? They might be, but this is not the case if mammals have been nibbling on your crop. Shockingly, eating a raw slug or snail can also impact your health in rare cases. Read more now to learn when it is okay to eat greens from the garden that have been damaged by insects.

The post Are Leafy Greens with Insect Holes Safe to Eat? appeared first on Gardener's Path.

item.title: Artichokes: All About California’s Official State Vegetable
item.description:

item.title: How to Grow and Care for Japanese Honeysuckle
item.description:
While Japanese honeysuckle is a favorite for pollinators, and known for being tolerant of neglect, it also has a reputation for unbridled growth and spreading out of control. In some areas, planting is discouraged or outlawed, but if you’re in a “safe zone,” this vine can bring the fragrance of summer to your garden.

The post How to Grow and Care for Japanese Honeysuckle appeared first on Gardener's Path.
item.source:markdown:
While Japanese honeysuckle is a favorite for pollinators, and known for being tolerant of neglect, it also has a reputation for unbridled growth and spreading out of control. In some areas, planting is discouraged or outlawed, but if you’re in a “safe zone,” this vine can bring the fragrance of summer to your garden.
The post [How to Grow and Care for Japanese Honeysuckle](https://gardenerspath.com/plants/vines/grow-japanese-honeysuckle/) appeared first on [Gardener's Path](https://gardenerspath.com).
